summary: All 5 workers audited telemetry contract across state-shape, monitor-consumption, atomic-write-patterns, launch-proof-counters, and result-bundle-paths. Found consistent atomic write patterns, documented state schema in commander agent, but identified critical gaps: monitor ignores child-agents.jsonl entirely, launch-proof validation is display-only and does not enforce 250-worker full deployment requirement, and documentation does not cover commanders/ subdirectory structure.
consensus_findings: Atomic write patterns are correctly implemented using POSIX rename semantics (tmp+mv) for swarm-state.json and O_APPEND for child-agents.jsonl appends.
Commander agent documentation is authoritative schema spec for swarm-state.json structure.
Monitor reads only swarm-state.json for all telemetry — child-agents.jsonl is written but never consumed by monitor.
Launch proof counters (squad_leads_started, workers_started) are display-only in monitor; they do not determine completion/partial status.

Full 250-worker deployment requirement is not validated — monitor will show COMPLETE even if workers_launched < 250, violating README requirement that metaswarm must report partial rather than silently downgrade.
Bundle writes to dual locations (progress_dir/bundle.json AND results/) but this is not documented.
critical_gaps: Monitor does not enforce the full 250-worker swarm requirement documented in README:130 and AGENTS.md. Any run completing all tasks shows COMPLETE regardless of worker proof.
child-agents.jsonl telemetry is write-only from monitor perspective — launch events logged but never read for validation or display.
Schema validation is absent — malformed swarm-state.json is silently ignored with fallback defaults, masking contract drift.
Documentation gap: README does not document commanders/ subdirectory or dual bundle.json write locations.
Naming ambiguity: launch_proof.squad_leads_started vs telemetry.squad_leads_launched — mapping not explicit.
